title: MongoDB Q1 FY2022 Earnings Call Transcript
text: MongoDB delivered strong Q1 FY2022 performance with revenue of $181.6 million, representing 39% year-over-year growth and exceeding guidance. Subscription revenue reached $174.6 million, up 40% YoY, while Atlas revenue surged 73% to become the majority of total revenue at 51%. The company celebrated Atlas reaching a significant milestone: becoming the majority of revenue in just under five years. Atlas now operates as a nearly $400 million revenue run rate business growing over 70% year over year, deployed across over 80 regions around the world across AWS, Azure, and GCP. MongoDB expanded its customer base to over 26,800 total customers, up from 18,400 a year prior. The company saw 1,057 customers with $100,000+ ARR, compared to 780 in the prior year. The net ARR expansion rate remained above 120%. Full-year revenue outlook: $771–$784 million. Operating loss: $76–$68 million (non-GAAP). Net loss per share: $1.38–$1.25 (non-GAAP). Management emphasized that two-thirds of new business won by field sales was Atlas, demonstrating strong enterprise adoption of the cloud offering over traditional Enterprise Advanced licensing.
